items.headline: City approves Seeds of Peace Monument artwork at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument honors survivors and represents a public art addition to one of the city's major parks.

items.headline: Council proposes changes to mayor and district office budget rules
items.summary: The city council is considering amendments to City Council Policy 1-18, Section 20, which governs how mayor and city council district office budgets are handled during transition years. The changes may affect how discretionary funding is allocated during leadership transitions.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan (2015–2040 Community Vision), which sets policy direction for public health, emergency preparedness, hazard mitigation, and safety infrastructure across the city over the next 14 years.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The Milpitas City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager on May 19, 2026. This fills the leadership position responsible for overseeing city operations and administration.

items.headline: New duplex permitted on Old Piedmont Road
items.summary: A new two-story, two-unit duplex has been permitted at 2174 Old Piedmont Road, representing a notable addition to San José's housing stock. At just over $1 million, this is the only multi-unit residential project in this permit batch.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates water shortage contingency plan
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed a draft Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. This plan outlines how the city will manage water resources and respond to drought conditions.

items.headline: Spring community grants and events approved
items.summary: City Council considered 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications in the spring funding cycle on May 18. Residents and nonprofits can apply for funding to support local events and neighborhood initiatives.

items.headline: City to set principles for residential electrification programs
items.summary: Palo Alto will consider proposed principles to guide residential electrification programs designed to help the city comply with regional air quality regulations. The city is developing a status update on electrification efforts and seeking to establish a framework for these programs.
